description_long: This triumphal arch features a single, large stone archway supported by two robust rectangular columns, each constructed from light gray masonry blocks. The arch is topped with a smooth, rounded keystone and is accented by dark, blocky capitals at the top of each column. The base consists of a series of wide, dark stone steps leading up to the arch, providing a sense of elevation and grandeur. The structure is devoid of decorative reliefs or sculptural embellishments, emphasizing a minimalist, utilitarian design. The stonework appears weathered, suggesting durability and historical significance. The open central passageway allows for easy traversal beneath the arch.
description: A large, stone triumphal arch with a rounded top, supported by two rectangular columns and set atop wide, dark steps. The design is minimalist, with no visible decorative elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the steps, both columns, and the complete archway.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the steps and the thickness of the arch, with one column mostly visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the other side of the arch and the steps' extension.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the back of the arch and steps, mirroring the front but with less detail on the step faces.
